zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Wotton於1428年出生在英國英格蘭爾切斯特布拉可時，她的父親Thomas de Wotton28歲，母親Joan Corbie24歲。她於大約1447年與Hugh Davenport III在英國英格蘭爾亨波瑞結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1483年2月16日在英國爾亨波瑞享年55歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Wotton于1428年出生在英国英格兰爾切斯特布拉可时，她的父亲Thomas de Wotton28岁，母亲Joan Corbie24岁。她于大约1447年与Hugh Davenport III在英国英格兰爾亨波瑞结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1483年2月16日在英国爾亨波瑞享年55岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Wotton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Wooten
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called with Old English wid(u), wudu ‘wood’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, such as Wootton in Northamptonshire or Oxfordshire, Wootton Bassett in Wiltshire, Wotton in Surrey, and Wotton under Edge in Gloucestershire.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Wotton was born in 1428, in Blacon, Chester, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Thomas de Wotton, was 28 and her mother, Joan Corbie, was 24. She married Hugh Davenport III about 1447, in Henbury, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 16 February 1483, in Henbury, Cheshire, England, at the age of 55.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Wotton est née en 1428, à Blacon, Chester, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas de Wotton, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Joan Corbie, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Hugh Davenport III environ 1447, à Henbury, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 16 février 1483, à Henbury, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 55 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Wottonが1428年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアチェスターブラコンで生まれた時，父Thomas de Wottonは28歳，母Joan Corbieは24歳であった。約1447年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアヘンブリーでHugh Davenport IIIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1483年2月16日にイギリスチェシュアイアヘンブリーで55歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Wotton(이)가 1428년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 체스터 블라콘에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas de Wotton(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Corbie(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 대략 1447년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 헨버이에서 Hugh Davenport III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1483년 2월 16일 영국 체실르 헨버이에서 55세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Wotton родилась в 1428 г. в местности блакон, честир, чешир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Thomas de Wotton было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Corbie, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hugh Davenport III около 1447 г. в местности хенбури, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла 16 февраля 1483 г. в местности хенбури, чешир, Англия в возрасте 55 лет.
